sections.paragraph:
ἐπίμονος, ον (ἐπιμονή ‘delay’, μονή ‘tarrying’, s. μένω; Clearchus, Fgm. 24 p. 17, 15; Polyb. 6, 43, 2 ἀκμαί; Plut., Mor. 166c κολαστήριον; SIG 679, 80 [143 b.c.]; Dt 28:59 Sym.; Philo, Ebr. 21) pert. to continuing for a relatively long time, lasting ἡ στάσις the rebellion continues 1 Cl 46:9. ἡ ἐ. κόλασις continuous torture MPol 2:4.—DELG s.v. μένω.

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 376
